body:
Hey Brian! I know this is kind of last minute, but I have a family thing come up on Thursday and I was wondering if you'd be able to cover my 7a-7p? I can take your Friday shift in return — I already checked the schedule and it looks like it should work out.

Let me know if that works for you. I'll update the board and notify charge if you're good with it.
snippet: Hey Brian! I know this is kind of last minute, but I have a family thing come up...
subject: Shift swap request

body:
Hey Dani, no worries at all! Thursday works for me and I actually don't mind picking up the extra day. Friday is fine too — I'll let charge know and we can swap on the schedule app.

Hope everything is okay with your family. See you around!

Brian
snippet: Hey Dani, no worries at all! Thursday works for me and I actually don't mind pic...
subject: Re: Shift swap request

body:
Hey babe, I was looking at the stack of mail and saw the renewal notice for your nursing license. Didn't you say you still needed a few more CE hours before the deadline? Just wanted to make sure you were on top of it — I know how crazy your schedule has been lately.

Let me know if you need me to look something up for you.
snippet: Hey babe, I was looking at the stack of mail and saw the renewal notice for your...
subject: Continuing education credits

body:
Ugh, thanks for the reminder. I need 6 more hours and the deadline is the end of next month. I signed up for an online ethics module last week but haven't started it yet.

I'll try to knock out a couple hours this weekend when the kids are at your mom's. Can you remind me Saturday morning?

Brian
snippet: Ugh, thanks for the reminder. I need 6 more hours and the deadline is the end of...
subject: Re: Continuing education credits

body:
Saturday morning reminder is already set on my phone. I also found a free 2-hour course on medication safety through the state nursing board website — I'll text you the link tonight.

You've got plenty of time, don't stress. Just don't leave it until the last week again like you did in 2023 😄
snippet: Saturday morning reminder is already set on my phone. I also found a free 2-hour...
subject: Re: Continuing education credits

body: Hey, just checking in. You said you might be home by 8 but it's almost 9 and I haven't heard from you. Kids are in bed, dinner is in the fridge. Everything okay over there?
snippet: Hey, just checking in. You said you might be home by 8 but it's almost 9 and I h...
subject: Charge nurse note

body:
So sorry — charge called out sick and I had to hold over to do handoff and finish up some incident documentation. I'm literally just wrapping up now. Should be out the door in 20 minutes.

Thank you for handling bedtime. I owe you big. Don't wait up!

Brian
snippet: So sorry — charge called out sick and I had to hold over to do handoff and finis...
subject: Re: Charge nurse note

body:
Oh no, I'm sorry. I left the soup on the stove on low so it'll be warm when you get here. The kids were fine — Sofia wanted to wait up for you but she fell asleep around 8:30.

Drive safe please. And actually eat something this time.
snippet: Oh no, I'm sorry. I left the soup on the stove on low so it'll be warm when you ...
subject: Re: Charge nurse note

body:
Just got in. The soup was exactly what I needed, thank you. Give Sofia a kiss from me in the morning.

Going to try to wind down and get to bed soon. Today was a lot but I'm okay. Love you.

Brian
snippet: Just got in. The soup was exactly what I needed, thank you. Give Sofia a kiss fr...
subject: Re: Charge nurse note

body:
Hey Brian, I know we talked briefly before shift change but I wanted to follow up on the situation with Mr. Ellison's family. His daughter stopped by again this afternoon asking a lot of questions about his pain management plan and I could tell she wasn't fully satisfied with my answers.

Did you have a chance to speak with her during your shift yesterday? She mentioned your name specifically.
snippet: Hey Brian, I know we talked briefly before shift change but I wanted to follow u...
subject: Patient family follow-up

body:
Yes, I spoke with her for a while yesterday — she was understandably anxious. I walked her through the current pain protocol and explained why the team had adjusted the dosing. I think she felt a little better after we talked, but she still has a lot of concerns.

I'd suggest looping in the patient advocate or asking the attending to schedule a family meeting. She really needs to hear directly from the doctor.

Brian
snippet: Yes, I spoke with her for a while yesterday — she was understandably anxious. I ...
subject: Re: Patient family follow-up

body:
That's a good call. I'll bring it up with the attending on rounds tomorrow and push for a family meeting. Having it documented that we've escalated will also help protect us if she continues to push back.

Did she say anything to you about filing a complaint? She mentioned something vaguely to me but I wasn't sure if she was serious.
snippet: That's a good call. I'll bring it up with the attending on rounds tomorrow and p...
subject: Re: Patient family follow-up

body:
She didn't say it directly to me, but I got the sense she was frustrated enough to go that route. I'd make sure everything is charted thoroughly — the education you provided, when she visited, what was discussed. Cover your bases.

Also heads up: she asked about speaking with the charge nurse, so they may have already been contacted. Worth checking with charge before your next shift.

Brian
snippet: She didn't say it directly to me, but I got the sense she was frustrated enough ...
subject: Re: Patient family follow-up

body:
I'll go through my charting tonight and make sure it's complete and timestamped. And I'll check with charge first thing tomorrow.

Thank you so much for the heads up — and for talking to her yesterday. You always handle those conversations so well. I'm still working on it. Let's debrief after shift this week if we get a chance.
snippet: I'll go through my charting tonight and make sure it's complete and timestamped....
subject: Re: Patient family follow-up

body:
Brian!! Are you working days or nights this week? I have Tuesday and Wednesday free and I'm desperate for a real catch-up lunch — not just texting each other memes in the parking lot of Target 😂

Let me know what works and I'll pick the spot.
snippet: Brian!! Are you working days or nights this week? I have Tuesday and Wednesday f...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Haha I feel called out and I deserve it! I'm off Tuesday actually — let's do it. Noon-ish works for me. Can we do that Thai place on Merrimon? I've been craving the drunken noodles for like two weeks straight.

So excited to actually sit down and talk like humans for once. See you Tuesday!

Brian
snippet: Haha I feel called out and I deserve it! I'm off Tuesday actually — let's do it....
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey! So I know we literally just had lunch but I've been thinking about what you mentioned about the work stress situation and I feel like we barely scratched the surface. Are you free Tuesday morning for coffee? Like 9am before the day gets away from us?

I just want to actually check in properly. No agenda, just us.
snippet: Hey! So I know we literally just had lunch but I've been thinking about what you...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
You are so sweet, and yes — I'd love that. I have a dentist appointment at 11 but 9am is perfect. That little coffee shop on Lexington? The one with the good lattes and the weird taxidermy?

And yes, I do have more to say. You know me too well.

Brian
snippet: You are so sweet, and yes — I'd love that. I have a dentist appointment at 11 bu...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
The taxidermy café it is 😂 Perfect, I'll be there at 9. And I'm buying — no arguments.

I'm really glad you said yes. I've been a little worried about you and I just want to hear how you're actually doing. No pressure to get into anything heavy, but I'm here. See you Tuesday ❤️
snippet: The taxidermy café it is 😂 Perfect, I'll be there at 9. And I'm buying — no argu...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Okay I am officially taking over your birthday planning because I know if I leave it to you it will be a shift at work and a sad gas station cupcake 😂 I'm thinking Saturday the 12th, brunch at Tupelo Honey — just a small group, you, me, Neil, maybe two or three others you actually like.

Are you free? Is Neil free? I'll handle reservations once I get the green light.
snippet: Okay I am officially taking over your birthday planning because I know if I leav...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I'm crying at 'sad gas station cupcake' because you're not entirely wrong 😭 I actually do have that Saturday off and Neil has no plans, so yes yes YES. Tupelo Honey is perfect.

Invite Dani if you want — she's been a really good friend at work lately and I think you two would get along great.

Brian
snippet: I'm crying at 'sad gas station cupcake' because you're not entirely wrong 😭 I ac...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Dani is absolutely in, I'll reach out to her! I'm going to book for 6 just to be safe. I was also thinking we could do something after — walk around downtown or grab dessert somewhere? Nothing huge, just stretch the fun a little.

Any dietary things I should know about for the group? And please do NOT tell me what you want as a gift. I already know and I'm not telling you what it is.
snippet: Dani is absolutely in, I'll reach out to her! I'm going to book for 6 just to be...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I am incredibly suspicious of you right now 😂 Neil is gluten-free, that's the only thing. And yes to walking around after — that sounds really lovely honestly.

Jess you really don't have to do all this. But also please never stop, because you make everything feel so special. I love you, thank you.

Brian
snippet: I am incredibly suspicious of you right now 😂 Neil is gluten-free, that's the on...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Hey, the home warranty renewal came in the mail. It's going up $18 a month this year which I think is a bit much. I've been looking at a couple of other providers and found one that looks comparable for about $30 less per month, but the reviews are more mixed.

Do you want to just renew with the current company or should we actually consider switching? I want your input before I do anything.
snippet: Hey, the home warranty renewal came in the mail. It's going up $18 a month this ...
subject: Renewal options

body:
Hmm, $30 less per month is like $360 a year so that's not nothing. What are the mixed reviews about — service response time or actual coverage gaps? Those are very different problems.

Can you send me the comparison you put together? I'd like to look at what's actually covered before we decide.

Brian
snippet: Hmm, $30 less per month is like $360 a year so that's not nothing. What are the ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Mostly service response time complaints — people saying it took over a week to get someone out for HVAC issues. Coverage-wise it looks pretty similar to what we have now. I'll forward you the side-by-side.

My gut is to stick with our current company since we've always had good experiences, but I wanted to hear your take first.
snippet: Mostly service response time complaints — people saying it took over a week to g...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
After looking at the comparison I think you're right — slow response time is a dealbreaker for me. What if the AC goes out in July and we're waiting a week? That's not a tradeoff I want to make.

Let's renew with the current company. Go ahead and take care of it whenever you have a few minutes. Thank you for doing all this legwork.

Brian
snippet: After looking at the comparison I think you're right — slow response time is a d...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Done! Renewed for another year. I also asked them to send the updated policy documents to both of our emails. I'll save everything in the shared folder when they come in.

And you're welcome — you deal with enough all day. The least I can do is handle the boring insurance stuff 😊
snippet: Done! Renewed for another year. I also asked them to send the updated policy doc...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Hey, we got an invoice from the landscaping company for $340 and I'm pretty sure they quoted us $275 for the spring cleanup. I don't have the original quote email handy right now — do you remember the number they gave us, or do you have the email somewhere?

I want to reach out to them before I pay anything.
snippet: Hey, we got an invoice from the landscaping company for $340 and I'm pretty sure...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
I'm pretty sure it was $275 too, but let me check my email when I get a break. I think Marcus sent the quote in March — search your inbox for 'Green Valley' and it should come up.

Don't pay it yet until we confirm. Could be a billing error but definitely worth a conversation first.

Brian
snippet: I'm pretty sure it was $275 too, but let me check my email when I get a break. I...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body:
Hey so I heard back from Dave at the management office. They're offering a 12-month renewal at $1,785 (up from $1,720) or a month-to-month option at $1,900. I also asked about a 6-month term and they said they don't offer that anymore.

We need to let them know by the 15th. What are you thinking?
snippet: Hey so I heard back from Dave at the management office. They're offering a 12-mo...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Month-to-month at $1,900 is a big jump for the flexibility. If we're not planning to move in the next year I think the 12-month makes the most sense — $65 more a month versus $180 more is a pretty significant difference.

Are you still thinking we might look for a house next year, or has that timeline shifted? That's really the main variable here.

Brian
snippet: Month-to-month at $1,900 is a big jump for the flexibility. If we're not plannin...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Honestly the house search timeline has shifted a bit — I don't think we'll realistically be ready before next spring at the earliest. So let's just do the 12-month renewal and revisit the house hunt in the fall when things settle down.

I'll email Dave today to confirm and ask him to send over the updated lease so we can review it before signing. Thanks for helping me think through it — I was going in circles.
snippet: Honestly the house search timeline has shifted a bit — I don't think we'll reali...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Hey! I know you subscribed to that nursing newsletter but you never actually read it 😄 I skimmed it this morning and pulled out the articles I thought you'd actually care about.

One is about updates to sepsis screening protocols — might be relevant to what you were telling me about last week. The other is an opinion piece on nurse-to-patient ratios in mid-size hospitals. Both seem worth a quick read.
snippet: Hey! I know you subscribed to that nursing newsletter but you never actually rea...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
I love that you read my nursing newsletter for me, that is either very sweet or deeply concerning and I haven't decided which 😂

The sepsis screening one does sound relevant — we've been having some internal conversations about early recognition. I'll read it on my lunch break tomorrow. Can you forward just those two articles?

Brian
snippet: I love that you read my nursing newsletter for me, that is either very sweet or ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Just forwarded both! The sepsis one is short, maybe 5 minutes. The ratio piece is longer but I have a feeling you'll have a lot of opinions about it.

Also there was a third article about burnout in night shift nurses but I didn't send it because I didn't want to be passive aggressive 😇
snippet: Just forwarded both! The sepsis one is short, maybe 5 minutes. The ratio piece i...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Oh my god please send me the burnout one immediately, you absolute menace 😂 I'm genuinely curious what they say.

Read the sepsis article at lunch — actually really good, made me rethink some things about early flagging. Thanks for the curation service, I clearly needed an editor.

Brian
snippet: Oh my god please send me the burnout one immediately, you absolute menace 😂 I'm ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Hey, can we figure out the pickup schedule for this week? I have an early meeting Thursday that might run until 6, which is past when Marcus gets out. And Sofia has dance on Tuesday and Thursday at 5:30.

I can handle Monday and Wednesday no problem. Just need help with Tuesday dance, Thursday school pickup, and maybe Friday depending on your shift.
snippet: Hey, can we figure out the pickup schedule for this week? I have an early meetin...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Okay let me lay this out: I can do Tuesday — I'll take Sofia straight from school to dance dropoff. Thursday school pickup is fine too since I'm on days this week and should be out by 4. Friday I'm on nights so I won't be available after 6pm.

For Friday, can your mom help? Or I can ask Dani — she lives near the school and has offered before.

Brian
snippet: Okay let me lay this out: I can do Tuesday — I'll take Sofia straight from schoo...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
I'll call my mom about Friday — I think she's free. If she can't I'll try to move my Friday afternoon call instead.

So to confirm: I have Monday and Wednesday, you have Tuesday and Thursday, Friday is (still working that out) pending my mom. Does that work on your end?

Also — do you know if Sofia needs her regular dance bag or her recital bag for Tuesday? She told me two completely different things and I've given up trying to figure it out 😅
snippet: I'll call my mom about Friday — I think she's free. If she can't I'll try to mov...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Yes that schedule works, confirmed on my end. And it's the regular dance bag for Tuesday — recital isn't until the 19th. I'll stick a note on it so there's no confusion.

Also please pack her a snack. She always comes out of dance absolutely starving and then gets dramatic about it in the car 😂

Brian
snippet: Yes that schedule works, confirmed on my end. And it's the regular dance bag for...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Snack is noted and will not be forgotten 😂 My mom just texted back — she can do Friday pickup no problem. She wants to take them for ice cream after so the kids are going to lose their minds.

I'll put the whole week in the shared calendar tonight. You are so good at logistics even when you're running on fumes. Thank you.
snippet: Snack is noted and will not be forgotten 😂 My mom just texted back — she can do ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Hey so I started looking at beach rentals for August. Two options I like: one in Folly Beach that sleeps 6 and has a pool, $2,400 for the week. And one right on the beach in Hilton Head, no pool, $3,100 for the week.

Both are available the week of August 10th. Which direction are you leaning? I need to book soon — these go fast.
snippet: Hey so I started looking at beach rentals for August. Two options I like: one in...
subject: Family vacation planning

body:
For me the pool is a big plus with the kids — Marcus would live in it and it gives us options on days when we don't feel like hauling everyone down to the beach. The $700 difference is real but so is our sanity 😂

I'm leaning Folly Beach. Can you check if it's pet-friendly in case we bring Rosie? Book it if everything looks good — I trust you completely.

Brian
snippet: For me the pool is a big plus with the kids — Marcus would live in it and it giv...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body:
Hey, what are you thinking for this weekend? I feel like we haven't had a low-key one in forever. My vote is farmers market Saturday morning and then a family movie night Saturday evening. Sunday could stay open — or I could finally tackle the garage if you want something productive.

What does your schedule look like?
snippet: Hey, what are you thinking for this weekend? I feel like we haven't had a low-ke...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
Farmers market and movie night sounds honestly perfect. I'm off both days for once so I am fully in.

For Sunday — can we do the garage together? It'll go so much faster with two people and then we can feel good about ourselves the rest of the day. Maybe grill out Sunday evening to celebrate surviving it?

Brian
snippet: Farmers market and movie night sounds honestly perfect. I'm off both days for on...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
Yes to all of this! Grilling Sunday evening is a great call — I'll grab everything when I do the weekly shop on Friday.

I'll let the kids pick the movie for Saturday. Fair warning: it will almost certainly be something we've both already seen at least 40 times 😂 See you tonight, love you.
snippet: Yes to all of this! Grilling Sunday evening is a great call — I'll grab everythi...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body: Confirmed the swap on the board — you take my Friday, I take your Sunday. On-call bag reminder set.
